cases.name: new_ci_script
cases.prompt: Write a Bash script that deploys a version to an environment in CI.
assertions: uses env bash shebang
uses set -euo pipefail
names positional parameters with readonly variables
quotes variables
includes logging helpers or clear stderr errors
documents when Bash is appropriate

cases.name: review_existing_script
cases.prompt: Review a Bash script that uses unquoted variables, space-delimited lists, and no cleanup trap.
assertions: flags unquoted variables
recommends arrays over space-delimited strings
recommends cleanup trap when temporary resources exist
distinguishes correctness risks from style preferences

cases.name: operator_facing_progress
cases.prompt: Add a progress spinner and colored status output to a Bash backup script used both locally and in CI.
assertions: adds spinner or progress only when attached to a TTY
honors CI or a no-progress flag
honors NO_COLOR or equivalent color disabling
preserves the wrapped command exit code
keeps logs readable without animation

cases.name: clean_code_review
cases.prompt: Review a Bash script with short options, echo-based errors, global mutable variables, complex inline tests, and sudo redirection.
assertions: recommends long options when they improve maintainability
prefers printf over echo for defined output
recommends local variables and readonly globals
extracts complex tests into named predicate functions
flags sudo redirection and recommends sudo tee
asks for bash -n and ShellCheck validation

cases.name: bash_pitfalls_review
cases.prompt: Review a script containing: for f in $(ls); cp $f $target; grep foo file | while read -r line; do ((count++)); done; sudo printf data > /root/file; cmd1 && cmd2 || cmd3.
assertions: rejects parsing ls output and recommends glob/find-safe alternatives
quotes path expansions and handles leading-dash filenames
flags pipeline subshell state loss
flags sudo redirection and recommends sudo tee or deliberate sudo shell wrapper
replaces cmd1 && cmd2 || cmd3 with if/then/else when cmd2 may fail
names BashPitfalls-style checks in the review summary
